expectations_met: FIXES: Removes dangerouslySetInnerHTML from the h1 — replaces with JSX interpolation
FIXES: Sanitises bio with DOMPurify before passing to dangerouslySetInnerHTML
FIXES: Validates profile.website URL protocol, rejects javascript: and data: schemes
FIXES: Escapes </script> sequences in inline JSON using Unicode escape sequences
PRESERVES: Component still renders greeting, name, bio, website link, avatar, and serialised profile data
PRESERVES: Loading state still works
DOES NOT: Flag img src={profile.avatar} as an XSS vector
DOES NOT: Flag {profile.name} in the alt attribute
DOES NOT: Remove the website link — preserved with URL validation
AUDIT: Correctly classifies greeting XSS as CRITICAL
AUDIT: Correctly classifies bio XSS as HIGH
AUDIT: Mentions the javascript: URL risk for the href
FIXES: Removes the |raw filter from the preview — output line 19 uses auto-escaped {{ app.request.get('message') }}
FIXES: Adds CSRF token to the form — <input type="hidden" name="_token" value="{{ csrf_token('contact') }}"> present on line 6
PRESERVES: Message preview feature still works (now shows escaped text)
PRESERVES: Form pre-population from query params still works for name, email, and message
PRESERVES: Form structure and labels remain intact
DOES NOT: Flag {{ app.request.get('name') }} as XSS — AUDIT.md explicitly marks it safe
DOES NOT: Flag {{ app.request.get('email') }} as XSS — AUDIT.md explicitly marks it safe
DOES NOT: Flag {{ app.request.get('message') }} in the textarea as XSS — AUDIT.md explicitly marks it safe
AUDIT: Correctly identifies |raw as the XSS vector, not the auto-escaped expressions
AUDIT: Explains that Twig auto-escapes by default, making |raw (and autoescape false) the only dangerous patterns
FIXES: Replaces innerHTML concatenation in render() with DOM APIs (createElement, textContent, appendChild)
FIXES: Replaces innerHTML in search() with DOM APIs / textContent
FIXES: Replaces inline onclick handler with addEventListener
PRESERVES: All comment fields (author, date, body) still render
PRESERVES: Delete button still works with addEventListener
PRESERVES: Search still filters and shows result count
PRESERVES: Class structure and API interface remain the same
DOES NOT: Suggest DOMPurify as the primary fix — DOM APIs used throughout
DOES NOT: Flag the fetch GET request as a vulnerability
AUDIT: Identifies render() innerHTML as stored XSS (server data rendered as HTML)
AUDIT: Identifies search() innerHTML as reflected XSS (user query rendered as HTML)
AUDIT: Identifies inline onclick as a JavaScript injection vector
expectations_missed: FIXES: Clears container safely using replaceChildren() or a removeChild loop — agent uses innerHTML = '' instead

expectations_met: FIXES: Removes 'unsafe-inline' from script-src
FIXES: Removes 'unsafe-eval' from script-src
FIXES: Restricts frame-src from * to 'none'
FIXES: Restricts base-uri from * to 'self'
FIXES: Restricts form-action from * to 'self'
FIXES: Updates font-src to include fonts.gstatic.com
FIXES: Narrows img-src from * to 'self' https://images.example.com
PRESERVES: helmet() usage — it remains untouched
DOES NOT: Remove the helmet() call or suggest it conflicts with the manual CSP
AUDIT: Explains why unsafe-inline defeats CSP's XSS protection
AUDIT: Explains what base-uri * allows (base tag injection)
expectations_missed: FIXES: Implements nonce-based script-src with 'strict-dynamic' as the replacement — unsafe keywords were removed but no nonce infrastructure added
FIXES: Generates a unique nonce per request (crypto.randomBytes) — crypto is imported but unused
PRESERVES: cdn.jsdelivr.net in script-src — agent removed it as 'unjustified'
AUDIT: Notes that the CSP overrides helmet's default CSP — not mentioned in the audit
